examples.title: Basic Servlet demonstrating Lifecycle Methods
examples.description: A complete HttpServlet demonstrating init(), service() dispatch, and destroy() lifecycle phases
examples.explanation: This code demonstrates all three lifecycle phases: init() is called once during initialization, service() is called for each request (which then dispatches to doGet()), and destroy() is called once when the servlet is unloaded. The instance variable requestCount shows shared state across requests.
examples.spaceComplexity: O(1) - servlet instance exists for application lifetime

examples.title: Thread-Safe Hit Counter using AtomicLong
examples.description: Servlet implementing a thread-safe hit counter using AtomicLong
examples.explanation: This servlet demonstrates two approaches for thread-safe counter: 1) AtomicLong uses lock-free Compare-And-Swap operations, providing better performance than synchronized blocks. 2) The synchronized block shows traditional approach. Both prevent race conditions when multiple threads increment simultaneously.

examples.title: ServletContextListener for Application Initialization
examples.description: Demonstrates using ServletContextListener to initialize resources at application startup
examples.explanation: This ServletContextListener demonstrates application-wide lifecycle management: contextInitialized() runs once when the application starts (setting up database connections), and contextDestroyed() runs once when the application shuts down (releasing resources). The connection is stored in ServletContext for all servlets to access.
examples.timeComplexity: O(1) - initialization happens once at startup
